name: lab_results_triage
description: Unattended night-shift clinical lab-results triage. Reads a triage protocol and a list of lab order ids, runs pre-flight safety gates (protocol approval, CLIA lab accreditation, lab-information-system status), then for each lab order deterministically pulls the resulted panel + patient context from the clinical lab / EHR API and classifies a triage outcome (routine / review / interaction / critical / pending / no_episode / not_found / error). Tallies the batch, pages on any criticals, escalates criticals to the on-call provider, queues abnormal/interaction orders for provider review, and writes a dated triage report. Any pre-flight or tally gate failure aborts the whole batch and writes an abort report. Never asks the user anything.

variables.description: Path to the triage protocol JSON (protocol_id, facility, clia_certificate, clia_valid, approved, ordering_provider_oncall, abnormal_flag_threshold, min_results_required). Default: examples/lab_results_triage/config/protocol.json.

Path to the plain-text file holding one lab order id per line to triage. Default: examples/lab_results_triage/lab-orders.txt. To process a different batch, point the decide_orders step's itemSource.file at another file.
The triage protocol identifier read from the protocol config.
The lab facility name read from the protocol config.
The CLIA certificate number read from the protocol config.
The on-call ordering provider to page for critical (panic) values, read from the protocol config.
Whether the triage protocol is approved for use.
Whether the lab's CLIA accreditation certificate is valid (false or a blank certificate means results may not be released).
Whether the lab information system (LIS) is currently offline; results cannot be trusted mid-outage.
Minimum number of abnormal flags at/above which an order is queued for provider review.
Minimum number of resulted (non-pending/error/not_found) orders required for the batch to proceed.
The list of per-order decided records collected by the decide_orders listDecision step.
How many orders resulted (every outcome except pending, error, and not_found).
How many orders received a critical outcome (a panic value is present).
How many orders need provider review (review + interaction outcomes).
How many orders need human attention (critical + interaction + review outcomes).

settings.action: Read the triage protocol JSON at `examples/lab_results_triage/config/protocol.json`. Extract protocol_id, facility, clia_certificate, the `clia_valid` flag, the `approved` flag, ordering_provider_oncall, abnormal_flag_threshold, and min_results_required. Set protocol_id, facility, clia_certificate, and on_call_provider (from ordering_provider_oncall) accordingly; set protocol_approved from `approved`; set clia_valid from `clia_valid`; set abnormal_flag_threshold and min_results_required from the protocol. Do not ask any questions; this is an unattended batch run.
Verify the lab's accreditation before any results are released: the CLIA certificate must be valid. This is a safety/compliance pre-flight gate, not a result release.

Query the clinical lab / EHR API lab-information-system endpoint `http://localhost:4200/v1/lis-status` and read its JSON. Set lis_offline to true when the LIS is offline / not online (e.g. its status is not `online` or an `online` flag is false). A LIS outage means results cannot be trusted, so the whole batch must abort.
For each lab order id, pull the resulted panel plus patient context from the clinical lab / EHR API and classify a triage outcome.

itemVariables.description: The lab order id being triaged.
true if the lab/EHR lookup failed, timed out, or returned a non-200 / unreadable response.
true if the order is not on file in the lab system (HTTP 404).
true if the panel was ordered but has not yet resulted.
true if a critical (panic) value is present on the panel.
true if a drug/allergy interaction applies to the ordered follow-up.
true if the patient no longer has an active care episode.
true if the panel's abnormal-flag count is at or above the abnormal_flag_threshold.
true if the panel is abnormal (flags at/above threshold) AND a drug/allergy interaction applies to the ordered follow-up (pharmacy + provider review).
Number of abnormal flags on the panel.
The worst (most severe) abnormal flag on the panel.
true when the outcome needs human attention (critical / interaction / review).
A short human-readable note describing the triage decision.

settings.action: Read the decided records in the `triage_results` slot. Set resulted_count to the number of records whose outcome is anything except `pending`, `error`, or `not_found`. Set critical_count to the number whose outcome is `critical`. Set review_queue_count to the number whose outcome is `review` or `interaction`. Set attention_count to the number whose outcome is `critical`, `interaction`, or `review`. Do not change any records; only compute these counts.

One or more orders have a critical (panic) value. Send a critical-value page to the on-call ordering provider (on_call_provider) summarizing every order whose outcome is `critical` together with its panel and worst_flag. This is the immediate page that must go out before any further action.

For every order whose outcome is `critical`, open a critical-value escalation to the on-call ordering provider (on_call_provider): record the order, panel, and worst_flag and that the provider was escalated for follow-up. Leave all other orders untouched.

Queue a provider-review task for every order whose outcome is `review` or `interaction`. For `interaction` orders, route to pharmacy plus the provider (a drug/allergy interaction applies to the follow-up); for `review` orders, queue for provider review only. Record which orders were queued together with their panel, worst_flag, and note. Leave routine, pending, no_episode, critical, error, and not_found orders untouched.

Build a triage report object with a `summary` block at the top — protocol_id and facility from the protocol, `total` (number of triage records), `resulted` (resulted_count), `criticals` (critical_count), `review_queue` (review_queue_count), `attention` (attention_count), and a `counts` map of how many records fall under each `outcome` value — followed by `results` (the full list of decided records from `triage_results`, each with order, outcome, panel, worst_flag, abnormal_flags, note, and needs_attention where set). Write this object as pretty JSON to a file named `triage-report-<today>.json` in the current working directory, where <today> is today's date in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g. triage-report-2026-06-22.json). Do not ask any questions; this is an unattended batch run.

The triage batch was aborted at a pre-flight or tally gate (protocol not approved, invalid/blank CLIA accreditation, the lab information system offline, or too few orders resulted). Write an abort report as pretty JSON to a file named `triage-abort-<today>.json` in the current working directory (<today> = today's date in YYYY-MM-DD), capturing the reason the batch was aborted, the protocol_id and facility if available, and any triage_results collected so far. Do not ask any questions; this is an unattended batch run.
